programme.title: The Tall Man
programme.desc: A nurse's (Jessica Biel) young son is the latest victim in a series of small-town child abductions rumored to be orchestrated by an ominous figure known as "the Tall Man."

programme.title: The Last Thing Mary Saw

programme.title: Kindred
programme.desc: Plagued by mysterious hallucinations, a pregnant woman suspects that the family of her deceased boyfriend has intentions for her unborn child.

programme.desc: An English doctor (Peter Cushing) helps a lord and his bride (Stephanie Beacham) cope with a severed hand and a curse.

programme.title: The Other Lamb
programme.desc: A young woman born into an all-female cult begins to question the teachings of her leader.

programme.title: The Pact
programme.desc: Disturbing visions and her sister's disappearance lead a woman (Caity Lotz) to call a cop (Casper Van Dien) and a psychic to her childhood home.

programme.title: The Babadook
programme.desc: A troubled widow (Essie Davis) discovers that her son is telling the truth about a monster that entered their home through the pages of a children's book.

programme.title: Dark Summer
programme.desc: Alone under house arrest, a teenager (Keir Gilchrist) experiences a terrifying brush with the supernatural.

programme.title: Welcome to Mercy
programme.desc: Stricken with stigmata, Madaline travels to a convent where nothing is what it seems -- and her friend August is seemingly the only person she can trust. Together, they must confront the demons inside Madaline before she becomes the Antichrist.

programme.desc: When a widow ventures into a remote wilderness area with her son and best friend to spread her husband's ashes, a mysterious hiker begins dogging them and forces them to confront their deepest fears.

programme.title: Andhaa Kanoon

programme.title: Da Vinci: The Lost Treasure
programme.desc: Leonardo da Vinci is considered by many to be one of the greatest artists who ever lived. Yet his reputation rests on only a handful of pictures - including the world's most famous painting, the Mona Lisa. As the National Gallery in London prepares to open its doors on a remarkable exhibition of Leonardo's work, Fiona Bruce travels to Florence, Milan, Paris and Warsaw to uncover the story of this enigmatic genius - and to New York, where she is given an exclusive preview of a sensational discovery: a new Leonardo.

programme.title: Art That Made Us
programme.desc: In the 18th century, trade and conquest funds an explosion of creativity, and art stirs a growing social conscience.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: The first round of the quiz for students continues with teams representing the Open University and Newcastle University facing off for a place in round two. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Sitcom about an irrepressible snob. Hyacinth's quest for perfection is somewhat hampered by the dubious antics of the rest of her family.

programme.title: Sorry!
programme.desc: Sitcom. Timothy's handsome cousin Brinsley comes to stay - and supplants Timothy in Mother's affections.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: Simon Schama examines the turbulent years in Britain from 1649 to 1689, from Oliver Cromwell's republic to Charles II's restoration and James II's subsequent pro-Catholic rule from which he was quickly deposed. This is the dramatic story of the revolutionary period after the execution of Charles I, when Cromwell ruled with an iron hand and Charles II attempted to restore the lustre of the monarchy.

programme.title: Berlusconi: Condemned to Win
programme.desc: Silvio Berlusconi’s ambitious takeover of AC Milan reshapes the club and elevates his own profile, charting a path from footballing triumphs to the brink of political power.

programme.title: Saving Venice
programme.desc: How Venetians are turning to revolutionary engineering, restoration of the natural environment and knowledge from their past in order to save Venice.

programme.title: Scotland's Finest: The Story of the Highland Games
programme.desc: A journey into the history, pageantry and characters that have shaped a Scottish phenomenon. Acclaimed actor Bill Paterson narrates the astonishing story of the Highland Games. From the battling clans to Queen Victoria's infatuation with her Highland subjects, the games have become a symbol of community and identity in Scotland and all across the world.

programme.title: Genius of the Ancient World
programme.desc: In the final episode, Bettany travels to China on the trail of Confucius, a great sage of Chinese history whose ideas have fundamentally shaped the country of his birth for around 2,500 years.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: In the Lambeyeque valley in northern Peru lies a strange lost world - the forgotten ruins of 250 mysterious pyramids, including some of the biggest on the planet, colossal structures made out of mud bricks. Long ago, the Lambeyeque people were haunted by a terrible fear and believed that building pyramids was essential to their survival. Their obsession reached its height at a city called Tucume, an eerie place of 26 pyramids standing side by side, the last pyramids this civilisation created before they vanished forever. What was the fear that drove these people to build so many pyramids, what were they for and why did the whole civilisation suddenly vanish? This film captures the moments when archaeologists at the site uncovered a mass of bodies of human sacrifice victims, following a trail of clues into the dark story of Tucume. It recreates the strange rituals of the people of the valley, revealing a civilisation whose obsession to build pyramids eventually turned to horror, until Tucume finally vanished in a bloody frenzy of human sacrifice.

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britain's most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, on its 100th anniversary. He comes face to face with chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about shocking animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend us against terrorist attacks and rogue nations.

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: Series re-examining the Ruth Ellis case. Gillian Pachter takes a forensic look at the police investigation and discovers worrying assumptions and problematic omissions.

programme.title: Remembers...
programme.desc: Acclaimed screenwriter Stephen Poliakoff shares his memories of the BBC's 1980 television drama Caught on a Train, his Bafta award-winning collaboration with celebrated producer Kenith Trodd. The drama was based on a real-life encounter Stephen experienced with an angry fellow passenger on a train trip across Europe. It stars the legendary Dame Peggy Ashcroft and Michael Kitchen as strangers who clash at the start of their journey but come to an understanding by the time they reach their destination. Here, Stephen reveals how the production came together, why he was left thinking the drama may herald the end of his career, and which iconic James Bond villainess was almost cast as the leading lady.

programme.title: Playhouse
programme.desc: A young Englishman, travelling on the Ostend to Vienna express, shares a train compartment with an American girl and an elderly Viennese lady.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer, broadcaster and Newsnight arts correspondent Stephen Smith explores Renaissance Florence under the reign of Grand Duke Cosimo Medici. Cosimo's fledgling court prized the finer things in life and some of the greatest painters, sculptors and craftsmen in world history came to serve the Grand Duke. But successful courtiers had to have brains as well as brawn. The canniest of them looked to theorists like Niccolo Machiavelli for underhand ways to get ahead, whilst enlightened polymaths turned their minds to the heavens, and to ice cream.

programme.title: Inside Porton Down: Britain's Secret Weapons Research Facility
programme.desc: Dr Michael Mosley investigates Britain's most secretive and controversial military research base, Porton Down, on its 100th anniversary. He comes face to face with chemical and biological weapons old and new, reveals the truth about shocking animal and human testing, and discovers how the latest science and technology are helping to defend us against terrorist attacks and rogue nations.

programme.title: University Challenge
programme.desc: In another first-round match in the quiz competition for students, Coventry University makes its debut appearance against a team from Cardiff University, with a place in round two at stake. Jeremy Paxman asks the questions.

programme.title: Only Connect
programme.desc: Quiz show presented by Victoria Coren in which knowledge will only take you so far, as patience and lateral thinking are also vital. It is all about making connections between things which may appear, at first glance, not to be connected at all.

programme.title: Dam Busters Declassified
programme.desc: Martin Shaw takes a fresh look at one of the most famous war stories of them all. The actor, himself a pilot, takes to the skies to retrace the route of the 1943 raid by 617 Squadron which used bouncing bombs to destroy German dams. He sheds new light on the story as he separates the fact from the myth behind this tale of courage and ingenuity. Using the 1955 movie The Dam Busters as a vehicle to deconstruct the raid, he tries to piece together a picture of perhaps the most daring attack in the history of aviation warfare. Along the way, Shaw hears from the last RAF veteran of the raid, as well as a German survivor of the tsunami which resulted from the Moehne dam's destruction.

programme.title: The Great Escaper
programme.desc: Based on the true story of Bernard Jordan, an 89-year-old veteran of the D-Day landings, who in 2014 sneaked out of his seaside care home where he lived with his wife, Rene, to board a cross-channel ferry and attend the 70th anniversary commemoration in Normandy.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: Celia Imrie tells the story of Glenda Jackson, one of Britain’s greatest-ever acting talents and a double Oscar winner, who famously turned her back on her hugely successful screen career to pursue a life in politics. With interviews and rarely seen moments from the BBC’s archives, this programme explores Jackson’s career to gain insight into the choices and aspirations that drove her over the years. There's also a look at how those famous appearances alongside Morecambe and Wise showed that a world-renowned star, known for her serious roles and strong principles, was still capable of having fun and celebrating the ridiculous side of life.

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs
programme.desc: FBI trainee Clarice Starling, enticing insights from confined killer Hannibal Lecter, shares her nightmare memory in the hope of stopping a serial killer before his latest victim dies. Multi-award-winning thriller.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest news from the BBC.

programme.title: Talking Business
programme.desc: Water has been a flashpoint in the Middle East and worldwide demand is outstripping supply, so how do we make sure there's enough to go round? This most basic resource is vital for drinking, sanitation and agriculture, but also for jobs and economies everywhere. The World Bank's global director for water Saroj Kumar Jha explains why they're trying to make it more of a priority for government spending. And Mareen Schneider, the founder and CEO of project developer Grasshopper Investments, explains how she's coming up with radical new ways for the private sector to invest in water. Plus, Ben Thompson also hears from José Manuel Entrecanales, the CEO of Spanish infrastructure giant Acciona, about the projects they're building around the world and why we need to rethink the value we give to water.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: AI Decoded speaks to leading tech podcaster Scott Galloway about the growing controversy surrounding Anthropic's latest AI model, Claude Mythos. During testing, the system uncovered thousands of hidden vulnerabilities in the software underpinning most of the world's computers - prompting Anthropic to decide it was too dangerous to release. The company says Mythos can outperform humans at certain hacking and cyber security tasks, raising fresh concerns among regulators, lawmakers and financial institutions about the risks to global digital infrastructure.

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as the results of this year’s council elections in England begin to flood in. Reeta Chakrabarti crunches the numbers at her giant screen, and Professor Sir John Curtice is on hand to offer his psephological knowledge and insight. BBC regional political editors are at the most important counts, and representatives of the main political parties give their reaction to the emerging political landscape.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Helen Skelton and Rav Wilding are joined by former detective Marc Cananur to discuss the warning signs of county lines exploitation, DIY expert Wayne Perrey looks at scaffolding safety and Dr Punam highlights symptoms you shouldn’t ignore. Also, online back button hijacking, an investing masterclass and Steve Backshall celebrates David Attenborough at 100.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team investigate the fraudulent websites convincing shoppers to invest in a ‘best-buy’ home appliance – and meet the woman who learned too late that her top-rated dehumidifier wasn’t up to the job. At the advice clinic in Mansfield, experts step in to help the couple denied boarding when their passports went missing at the airport. And in another how-to guide, consumer expert Harry Kind explains the steps to take when you’ve been involved in a collision on the road.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is in Wales, where a mixed commercial and residential unit is in surprisingly good condition. Jacqui’s house in Norwich is in similarly decent nick. But are they hiding any secret issues? Dion is in Staffordshire, where a three-bedroom mid-terrace has a quirky layout to contend with.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles heads to Sussex, where experts Raj Bisram and Nick Hall join the teams as they search for the best items to sell at auction in Newbury. Eric also sails along the Kennet and Avon Canal to learn about its fascinating 200-year history.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: In this episode, the team try to prevent a barber in Doncaster, who is actually working whilst on the phone, paying out to a tricksy scammer. As the scammer tries to persuade her that he is there to help recover some lost cryptocurrency, the ground team are on the road to her shop to put a stop to the scam. The team have their work cut out to persuade the barber and her colleagues that they are legit – and this particular race against time goes right to the wire.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Giant Sea Monster
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough investigates the discovery of a lifetime: the giant skull of a prehistoric sea monster, known as a pliosaur – the Tyrannosaurus rex of the seas!

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In true Escape to the Country style, today’s couple (both former international rugby players) want to leave London in search of a rural idyll in Dorset, and Alistair Appleton is the man to help them on their way. Aside from property, Alistair learns about the health benefits of watercress at a farm which specialises in growing this delicious and nutritious crop.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, flatmates Rachel, Scott, Tara and Daniel – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: The Silence of the Lambs

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: How much can you trust your MOT? In a special scams episode, Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the so-called ‘ghost’ MOTs available for sale – and hear from the mum whose second-hand car was far from roadworthy. The team reveal what happened when they tried to buy fake MOTs online. And the advice clinic heads to Reading to help the man who paid more than £200 for gift cards as Christmas presents for his family but discovered most of them had already been redeemed. The team’s research reveals which gift cards are most susceptible to being spent by scammers before customers have had a chance to use them.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is in London to meet a mum helping her daughter to get on the property ladder. Jacqui’s perplexed by the layout of two linked flats that she feels should just be one. And Tommy feels like he’s in a Swedish sauna, given the amount of timber inside a Staffordshire terrace.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Students take on lecturers in a battle of the brains! In red are friends Amy and Laura, who study history and maths – which they hope will be an ideal combination for bargain hunting! Their opponents in blue are Karen and Nick, who plan to ‘teach the reds a lesson’. With pride at stake, both teams dash around an antiques fair in Wetherby in search of three profitable items to sell at auction. Guiding the reds is Philip Serrell, who reckons he’s as old as some of the items his team are looking at! Maths undergraduate Laura isn’t impressed with a calculating machine, and the team turn up their noses at a perfume atomiser. Giving the blues an education in antiques is Jonathan Pratt. A rusty vintage biscuit tin gives them food for thought, but they decide a model of a Red Arrows jet won’t fly in the saleroom. While the teams shop, presenter Caroline Hawley is on home turf in Yorkshire as she quizzes fair-goers about a horsehair-stuffed mystery item! Over in Ripon, will it be 'neigh' or 'yay' from the bidders when the teams’ items go under the hammer?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: The team track a tech support scammer working out of New Delhi as he tries to steal money from a man in the UK. As the scammer remotely accesses the man’s computer, the team try desperately to get him on the phone and explain that his money is at serious risk. The man lives in a park home, which gives the team an opportunity. Can they convince the park’s employees to help? Also on the programme, we chat to a scammer posing as Jennifer Aniston.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Sea Dragon
programme.desc: A remarkable 200-million-year-old fossil - the bones of an ichthyosaur, a giant sea dragon - has been discovered on the Jurassic coast of Britain. David Attenborough joins the hunt to bring this ancient creature's story to life. Using state-of-the-art imaging technology and CGI, the team reconstruct the skeleton and create the most detailed animation of an ichthyosaur ever made. Along the way, the team stumble into a 200-million-year-old murder mystery. Only painstaking forensic investigation can unravel the story of this extraordinary creature's fate.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The devoted owners of no fewer than six Irish wolfhounds are in desperate need of more space for them and those countless muddy paws, so have set their sights on Suffolk for a bit more elbow room. Briony May Williams is there to guide them as they tour a selection of beautiful homes. Briony also gets to mess about on a stunning million pound, bespoke wooden yacht, a sister vessel to the ones featured in James Bond.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies

They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. programme.desc: The team revive a cherished rocking horse, a spectacular set of conga drums, and a logbook that played a key role in British LGBTQ+ history.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the high-stakes quiz, joined by a celebrity team featuring comedian and actor Phil Wang, dating expert Anna Williamson, actor and comedian Miles Jupp and actor Jamie-Lee O’Donnell as they take on the Bridge of Lies in support of their chosen charities. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: The team meets the couple who have been stuck in a battle with their insurer for more than three years over fixing damage to their home caused by subsidence. At the advice clinic in Reading, there is expert help for one man who suddenly discovered he had been paying a monthly book subscription that wasn’t even his for more than three years. Also on the programme, the renters stuck waiting for faster broadband because their landlords won’t approve the upgrade.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion encounters two front doors in Wigan and has a decision to make. Which one should he go through? Tommy sees a Grade II listed property in Norwich which is crying out for a sympathetic restoration. And Martin meets a London cabbie turned builder who has lofty ambitions for a flat in Kent.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Hello playmates! Presenter Danny Sebastian admires a collection of items which once belonged to comedian and actor Arthur Askey. They include medals for school sports and his long service to entertainment, as well as wristwatches and theatre dressing room door plaques. It’s a double bill at Newark antiques fair as two couples try to make showstopping purchases. Each pair has £300 in their pockets and an hour on the clock. On the red team are civil servants Sarah and Graeme, and in blue are Helen and Nick. Both teams show an early interest in furniture, with the reds drawn to an imposing desk, but expert Roo Irvine isn’t convinced. Over with the blues, a recent holiday to Kenya and Tanzania inspires Helen to consider a reclining stone hippo sculpture, but Caroline Hawley won’t let them wallow in hesitation for long. In Derby, James Lewis is on the rostrum, and there are hopes and regrets over purchases. Which team will come out on top?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Scam Interceptors
programme.desc: In the scam hub, the team are monitoring an incredibly slick scammer posing as payment provider PayPal as he tries to defraud a woman in Essex. Nick and Harleen desperately try to call and text the woman to stop her from continuing the scam, but the scammer is so professional, he is repeatedly able to keep her onside. The only option is to get ground interceptors Harriet and Meea out on the road to try and put this scam to an end face to face.

programme.title: Attenborough and the Mammoth Graveyard
programme.desc: Sir David Attenborough joins an archaeological dig uncovering Britain’s biggest mammoth discovery in almost 20 years. In 2017, in a gravel quarry near Swindon, two amateur fossil hunters found an extraordinary cache of Ice Age mammoth remains and a stone hand-axe made by a Neanderthal. Professor Ben Garrod joins the team at DigVentures during the excavation as they try to discover why the mammoths were here and how they died. Could the Neanderthals have killed these Ice Age giants?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Today’s escapees want to raise their children near the sea, so Jules Hudson takes them to view a selection of wonderful homes in the Devon and Dorset countryside. Away from houses, Jules takes to a three-wheeled bicycle when he joins a scheme that helps people to get out and about on the area’s beautiful coastline.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp hosts as a team of pop stars – Pussycat Doll Kimberly Wyatt, Claire Richards from Steps, Eternal’s Kéllé Bryan and Sugababe Amelle Berrabah – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie, and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash for their chosen charities.

programme.desc: It’s election day, and Ian and Elaine are anxiously awaiting the results. Nicola tries to support George, and Max’s jealousy mounts.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: With the leaderboard closer than ever, all four teams leave Zaamin National Park and race towards the next checkpoint, Almaty, Kazakhstan’s former Soviet-era capital.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The week closes with the third quarter-final as the six best cooks of the week return, taking on two more challenges to prove they have the nerve and skill to go even further.

programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon is back in the host’s chair for the award-winning comedy panel show, with Lee Mack and David Mitchell as the lightning-quick team captains. Each week, a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal extraordinary stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Sorting the fact from the fiction this week are guests Lady Leshurr, Philippa Perry, Alan Titchmarsh and Mike Wozniak.

programme.title: Election 26
programme.desc: Laura Kuenssberg is your guide as the results of this year’s council elections in England begin to flood in. Reeta Chakrabarti crunches the numbers at her giant screen, and Professor Sir John Curtice is on hand to offer his psephological knowledge and insight. BBC regional political editors are at the most important counts, and representatives of the main political parties give their reaction to the emerging political landscape.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir Dhondy reveals the realities behind modern Las Vegas. She goes to a high-octane pool party to discover how casinos are cashing in on the party scene.

programme.title: Sin City: The Real Las Vegas
programme.desc: Tir goes even further into the underbelly of Las Vegas to investigate how the reinvention of the city to pull in a new generation of visitors is affecting those who live and work there.

programme.title: Leigh-Anne: Race, Pop and Power
programme.desc: This powerful documentary follows pop star Leigh-Anne Pinnock as she confronts her experience as the only black member of Little Mix, and as a black woman in the music industry. Leigh-Anne talks about the racism she experienced growing up. Both her parents are of Caribbean heritage, and Leigh-Anne identifies as black. She is also aware that having lighter skin and being a celebrity means she is in a more privileged position than others. Leigh-Anne embarks on her own very personal journey to understand how she can use her platform and privilege to combat the profound racism she sees in society around her. After she begins her journey, news of George Floyd’s death and the Black Lives Matter protests start to sweep the world. With the force of a global movement now behind her, Leigh-Anne confronts those closest to her and attempts to bring difficult conversations about black representation right to the top of the music industry.

programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to Suriname, a country with more forest than anywhere else on earth. He kayaks upriver in search of some deadly critters.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads down in a deep-sea submersible to explore the ocean depths like never before. He then travels to the Natural History Museum to meet some of the weirdest animals from the deep.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: At the halfway stage of the race, the teams discover their next checkpoint lies within Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan. To reach it, they must navigate the endless horizons of the vast Kazakh steppe.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Liv hits the beach for her first ever date, while Charles goes sailing. Dianne meets her first match, and Olivia’s date makes a bold proposition.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Olivia invites her date to meet the family, and Liv shares a passionate moment on the harbour. Charles continues his search for love, and Dianne ends her first date on a high.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The eight remaining designers pair up to create uplifting spaces for a homelessness charity in London. They tackle an arts and crafts room, a group workshop room, a wellness area and an employment suite. The charity’s ambassador, Dara Ó Briain, joins Alan to check in on the designers before Michelle and guest judge Sophie Robinson critique the spaces.

programme.title: Kingdom
programme.desc: Leopard mother Olimba is bringing up her two cubs in an ideal leopard territory called Nsefu in Zambia. However, her world is soon turned upside down by the arrival of wild dog Storm and her chaotic pack. Storm seems unchallengeable – even dominating hyena Tandala and the rest of her clan. But just when the dogs are beginning to settle into the kingdom and welcome a litter of puppies, an even more powerful force appears on the horizon – lions. These new arrivals are a threat to everyone here – something that is brought into sharp focus when Olimba's young son goes missing...

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: In Senegal, west Africa, live a group of chimpanzees led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for three years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him defend his leadership. As the dry season sets in, the group are forced closer together to survive. But David is now surrounded by rivals who all want his crown and are prepared to kill him for it. David faces brutal battles, has his world engulfed in flames and has to pull off an extraordinary act of deception. In a story of power and politics, can David overcome the threats to his leadership and hold on to the alpha position long enough to sire a possible future heir to his throne?

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A mother puma must battle rivals, tackle prey nearly three times her size and endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in her bid to raise four cubs – her biggest litter yet.

programme.title: The Green Planet
programme.desc: More kinds of plants are crammed together in the tropical rainforests than anywhere else on Earth. The result is astonishing beauty and intense competition - a plant battleground. New filming techniques allow us to enter the plants’ world and see it from their perspective and on their timescale. From fast-growing trees to flowers that mimic dead animals, this is a journey into a magical world that operates on a different timescale to our own.

programme.title: Mammals
programme.desc: The dark, a world of opportunity for mammals. More than two-thirds prefer the cover of the night, with some now returning to the safety of the shadows in order to avoid us.

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve takes a special journey to meet the most trafficked animal on earth - the pangolin. He meets the people working tirelessly to save them.

programme.title: Women's Super League
programme.desc: Coverage of the Women's Super League match between Brighton & Hove Albion and Arsenal.

programme.title: Point Break
programme.desc: Action thriller. FBI special agent Johnny Utah goes undercover with a gang of surfers suspected of being the infamous bank robbers known as the 'Ex-Presidents'. But the closer he gets to the enigmatic leader Bodhi, the more his cover gets blurred. Is Johnny the cat or the mouse?

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Molly tries to make weight for her fight and avoid penalties, and her mum comes to Vegas for an emotional heart-to-heart. Paddy and friends cheer Molly on from home in Liverpool.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Molly wins her fight and comes back to Liverpool to see best mate Paddy, who also receives more concerning news about his shoulder injury which could stop his next fight.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy ‘The Daddy’ tries to find out if having a baby is as tough as fight camp by getting wired up to a contraction machine. And big news from his medical team changes everything.

programme.title: Paddy & Molly: Show No Mersey
programme.desc: Paddy and Molly reflect on the last few months and all their triumphs. Molly and Ellis visit a fertility clinic, and Paddy tries DIY for the first time as the babies are about to arrive.

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: In Senegal, west Africa, live a group of chimpanzees led by an alpha male named David. He has already been alpha for three years - a time when leaders here are usually overthrown. To make matters worse, David has no allies - no-one to help him defend his leadership. As the dry season sets in, the group are forced closer together to survive. But David is now surrounded by rivals who all want his crown and are prepared to kill him for it. David faces brutal battles, has his world engulfed in flames and has to pull off an extraordinary act of deception. In a story of power and politics, can David overcome the threats to his leadership and hold on to the alpha position long enough to sire a possible future heir to his throne?

programme.title: Dynasties
programme.desc: A mother puma must battle rivals, tackle prey nearly three times her size and endure the wild mountain weather of Patagonia in her bid to raise four cubs – her biggest litter yet.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Asia is the most mountainous continent on earth. From the forested peaks of the tropics to the hostile heights of the Himalayas, Asia's mountains are home to an astonishing abundance of specialised plants and wildlife. In India, elephants live in high-altitude tea plantations, and in Thailand, mountainous caves hide the planet's greatest gathering of swifts. In Pakistan, markhor live life truly on the edge, fighting for mates atop towering cliffs, and in Nepal, the forested foothills of the Himalayas provide hiding places for one of Asia's shyest mammals, the red panda.

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: As the team struggle to get used to their new surroundings, Freddie Flintoff pushes them to embrace the local culture and cuisine. After discovering Adnan is struggling with life on tour, Freddie gets the team to rally around, which requires making some unusual sacrifices. And with the next match approaching, their first training session reveals a promising new addition to the squad, who could give the team a fighting chance of securing their first victory in India.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: A devastating murder leaves the Innocents shocked. Players second-guess everyone's true identity, and trust becomes a thing of the past. Unaware of the Traitors' secret mission, the group ventures into a desolate no-man’s-land to build the prize pot. As tensions rise and suspicions grow, alliances are tested like never before. Consumed by paranoia, players struggle to collaborate. Can they look past their differences and focus on the real matter at hand - uncovering the Traitors? Or will the Traitors continue to rule this divided lot from the shadows?

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: The game of trust and betrayal gets tougher. This time, the players must choose between personal or collective gain. The mission forces them to second-guess even their most trusted allies and brings out their selfish agendas. As the day unfolds, the Innocents are left grappling with new theories and suspicions, and the true nature of the game continues to unravel. With every decision crucial, the stakes are higher than ever, and betrayal is always just around the corner. At the Circle, new accusations fly and old doubts resurface as the players attempt to uncover who can be trusted. Meanwhile, the Traitors face a tough choice in the secret tower.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: Morning brings good news, but as always, happiness is short-lived in the palace. Breakfast takes a dramatic turn as four players face an unexpected twist, leaving the entire group baffled. In the mission, the players channel the mindset of the Traitors to answer challenging questions. Racing against time, the group fights to save one player from an uncertain fate, but the mission’s high stakes push alliances to the breaking point. At the Circle of Shaq, a long-doubted player gets dragged back into the spotlight. And as the night ends, the Traitors take over the secret tower to seal the fate of one of the four targeted players.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Liv hits the beach for her first ever date, while Charles goes sailing. Dianne meets her first match, and Olivia’s date makes a bold proposition.

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Adam Henson and Sammi Kinghorn head to the Vale of Evesham, where fertile soils and a mild microclimate have long made it one of Britain’s most productive growing regions. Early spring brings the so-called hungry gap, but the vale has a long history of producing food, even in these leaner months. From the first spears of its world-famous asparagus to high-tech growers producing food year round, and from historic market gardens to traditional orchards alive with early pollinators, Adam and Sammi explore a landscape shaped by both tradition and innovation. While fresh produce may be scarce elsewhere, the Vale of Evesham is already beginning to flourish. Meanwhile, Charlotte Smith investigates how falling milk prices are leading some dairy farmers to call it a day, despite record exports and booming milk production.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: At the halfway stage of the race, the teams discover their next checkpoint lies within Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan. To reach it, they must navigate the endless horizons of the vast Kazakh steppe.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: There is lots of planting for the plate at Beechgrove Garden as Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham start growing a wide variety of edible produce. Kirsty is the first of the Beechgrove team to kick off work on her competition plot. Each presenter has been given a sense to appeal to in each of their competition borders, and Kirsty is planting things that will taste fantastic. She is also trying something new to Beechgrove: hops. Meanwhile, Brian provides an update on the veg he sowed at the start of the series and is in his rain garden checking on what survived this year's wet weather. There are all the usual handy hints, and there is a visit to Calum Clunie’s allotment in Leven.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his Japanese adventure, Michael explores the history of Hokkaido’s indigenous Ainu people. Travelling east to the Iburi region, an area closely associated with Ainu culture, he visits Lake Shikotsu. Over a traditional meal of salmon soup on the lake shore, he learns about the beliefs and values of the Ainu and how they were forced to assimilate to Japanese society following Hokkaido’s colonisation. After lunch, he is treated to a performance of the ancient Dance of the Cranes. Continuing north to Hokkaido’s capital, Sapporo, Michael enjoys the last evening of his journey in the Susukino district, a Japanese nightlife hotspot. Just along the coast from the port of Otaru on the Sea of Japan, Michael ends his tour with a trip to a famous distillery in the small town of Yoichi.

programme.title: Farm 999
programme.desc: In Warwickshire, farmers take a stand against fly-tippers who have been blighting their land with van-loads of dumped waste. When brothers Tom and Adam Beaty catch two men in the act, tensions rise and police rush to the scene – but will the offenders be held to account? In Northern Ireland’s Mourne mountains, fast-moving wildfires threaten grazing land and livestock, as exhausted firefighters and farmers join forces to bring the flames under control. And in north Wales, reporter Paul Colgan meets Rhianon Bragg, a courageous survivor of domestic abuse whose terrifying ordeal on her rural smallholding exposed the hidden dangers faced by victims living in isolation.

programme.title: Lost and Found in the Lakes
programme.desc: The freedivers take on a search at a castle’s jetty on Windermere, where five years ago a devastated couple lost their treasured wedding ring. Social media detective Mui gets creative in the hope of tracking down the owner of a phone found in the depths, and our crafter Lucy turns a medley of old bottles and jars pulled out of the lake into something quite magical.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined by award-winning photographer Becky Bailey, whose work capturing weddings has given her a unique window into the many forms that love can take. Together, they discuss relationships, communication and how connection evolves over time.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Game show in which 24 players compete across the series, scoring points by answering questions correctly. However, they must avoid the impossible answers, otherwise they will be eliminated from the show until the next day. In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a £10,000 question.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio. The contestants’ motivation in each round is simple: stay out of the light and you’ll be all right!

programme.title: Clive Myrie’s Caribbean Adventure
programme.desc: Clive travels 200 miles east of Havana to the historic town of Trinidad – a Unesco world heritage site. Clive meets the octogenarian carpenter whose skills have contributed to keeping this 16th-century city standing, and he learns how to salsa dance with the locals before putting his moves to the test at one of the town’s live music venues. Clive also discovers the dark history of sugar, which was once the main industry here.

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The team begins a six-month expedition tracking lions, leopards and cheetahs in the Okavango Delta. Wildlife cinematographer Gordon Buchanan follows one of the largest lion prides in Africa as they hunt to feed 28 hungry mouths, including tiny cubs. Anna Dimitriadis tracks down the Okavango’s most elusive big cat, the cheetah. And local wildlife filmmaker Brad Bestelink reunites with his favourite leopardess, Xudum. A huge wildfire threatens the resident big cats and the team.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and a team of experts invite members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. This programme comes from Mellerstain House in the Scottish Borders, where interesting finds include a valuable majolica dish, a dressing case, and a Lowry doodle.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Today Flog It is at the RAF Museum at Hendon in London. There's a photo album of RAF Hendon in the 30s and a rare picture that brings a surprise in the saleroom.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Jordan Banjo, Sian Lloyd, Nick Pickard and Ellie White take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman. A daily winner is declared following a quickfire round at the end of each show, and the scores are tallied across the week, resulting in an overall champion being crowned on Friday. Will the winning stars opt for the much-coveted House of Games wheelie luggage, or will they be tempted with the very popular bee hotel?

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael’s South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world’s largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung’s headquarters, tracing the rise of the family-run company from a small trading business to a global technology giant and symbol of Korea’s postwar economic miracle. Touring a futuristic smart home, Michael explores how innovation now shapes everyday life. Back in central Seoul, he visits the city’s original railway station, built during Japanese rule and now preserved as a cultural space, reflecting the complexities of Korea’s colonial past. In Gangnam, a district propelled to global stardom by a K-pop hit, he explores the international success of Korean popular culture, meeting the boss and dancers at a K-pop academy to understand how Hallyu – the Korean cultural wave – has conquered international audiences. Finally, in Seodaemun, Michael visits a former prison and Independence Park to uncover the story of the March First Movement of 1919, a pivotal moment in Korea’s struggle for independence from Japan, meeting a descendant of a leading resistance figure whose courage helped shape Korea’s struggle for freedom.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the upper reaches of the River Dee, which rises higher than any river in the UK. In the heart of the Cairngorm Mountains, he learns about writer and mountain lover Nan Shepherd’s passion for the living landscape. He then hears tales about a 100-year-old Jacobite and a Zeppelin airship that attacked a bare mountain. On Royal Deeside, Paul is intrigued by the story of John Brown - Queen Victoria’s very special friend, whose photograph and ring she took to the grave.

programme.title: Planet Earth II
programme.desc: A compilation of the wildlife documentary series presented by David Attenborough. A chance to enjoy the spectacular Planet Earth II, exploring wildlife dramas from across the globe. Ten years on from the groundbreaking original Planet Earth, Planet Earth II shows us life on our planet in entirely new ways. Captured in stunning ultra-high definition detail and using a whole range of new camera technology, this series brings us closer to animals than ever before and reveals new wildlife dramas for the very first time. Planet Earth II is an immersive exploration of the world's jungles, mountains, deserts, islands, grasslands and our newest habitat - cities. As our planet continues to change, never have these wildernesses been as fragile and as a precious as they are today. The series journeys to the four corners of the globe to explore the greatest treasures of our living planet and discover the remarkable ways animals overcome the challenges of surviving in the wildest places on Earth.

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: Sandi Toksvig takes a QI look at vices and virtues with Eshaan Akbar, Jo Brand, Rhys James and Alan Davies.

programme.title: This Is a Bomb: The Nevada Casino Heist
programme.desc: Unbeknownst to the FBI, a quiet Hungarian immigrant landscaper sits in his home, 250 miles away from Lake Tahoe, Nevada. He has no apparent ties to organised crime and no known record. In 1980, a booby-trapped bomb accompanied by a $3 million ransom note terrorises a casino in Lake Tahoe. Who is the calculating mind behind this sophisticated device and elaborate extortion plan, and what role do his sons play in their father’s dangerous game?

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the crucial news stories of the day.

programme.title: The People’s Piazza: A History of Covent Garden
programme.desc: David Olusoga tells the story of London’s Covent Garden Piazza. Helped by experts and eyewitnesses, he conjures up ghosts of the past - market traders, sex workers and activists.

programme.title: Waves
programme.desc: Tensions beneath the surface cause Florida teen Tyler to sink self-destructively, devastating those closest to him, including his sister Emily.

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at love and relationships in the deaf community, from a couple married for over 50 years to honest conversations about modern dating and finding connection today. Presenter Rolf Choutan is joined by award-winning photographer Becky Bailey, whose work capturing weddings has given her a unique window into the many forms that love can take. Together, they discuss relationships, communication and how connection evolves over time.

programme.title: Twenty Twenty Six
programme.desc: Sarah’s slip of the tongue on Alicia Byrd’s podcast means that she’s now gone viral as the ‘wooden condoms girl’. The oversight team decide it would be a good idea if she does a live interview on one of the many network news shows clamouring to meet her. But although Sarah agrees, she’s completely unprepared for the idea of millions of people watching her. Around the same time, rumours break on social media about a high-profile footballer, resulting in the team needing to draw up an official statement which everyone can agree on.

programme.title: Inside Barlinnie
programme.desc: Long-term addict Bobby heads to rehab, Billy fights for recognition that he has really changed, while Alan is released and heads back to his old ways on Glasgow's streets.

programme.title: This is BBC Two
programme.desc: Highlights of programmes on BBC Two.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Christina Trevanion buckles up in a vintage American muscle car to take a spin around one of Britain’s fastest racetracks. At 1.85 miles long, Castle Combe in Wiltshire is a popular circuit for vintage racing cars and has hosted some of the world’s motor racing greats. Christina rides shotgun with noise-monitoring officer Dave Hensley to learn about the history of the track and why drivers need to have their wits about them as they navigate its hair-raising straights and bends. Motoring around an antiques fair in Devon are two teams of families. In red are dad and daughter Alan and Maisie, guided by antiques expert Ben Cooper. Their opponents in blue are mum and daughter Denise and Amy, with expert Gary Pe. Alan kicks off the reds’ shop by picking out a contemporary glass vase, but Ben doesn’t see a profit in it. Meanwhile, a pair of miniature wooden doll's chairs catch Denise’s eye, but does Gary think they’ll bring a big return? Down the road from Castle Combe, auctioneer Tim Weeks gives Christina his estimates of the teams’ items. One bonus buy particularly impresses Tim, but will it fulfil its auction potential?

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael begins his journey at the Demilitarised Zone, the heavily fortified border that has divided the Korean peninsula since the end of the Korean War. From the track of a long-abandoned railway near the Imjin River, he explores one of the most tense frontiers in the world and reflects on a conflict that ended in ceasefire but never peace. From the DMZ, Michael travels south by rail into Seoul, arriving in a vast, fast-moving capital that has risen from the ruins of war to become one of the world’s most advanced cities. Exploring palaces and the restored Cheonggyecheon stream, he uncovers how tradition and innovation coexist in everyday Korean life. At the BBC’s Seoul bureau, Michael meets the news editor broadcasting news into North Korea, before sharing street food with a North Korean defector who poignantly describes the personal cost of division. Michael’s first Korean leg ends at Kukkiwon, the World Taekwondo Headquarters, where he discovers how Korea’s national martial art reflects discipline, resilience and a hard-won modern identity.

programme.title: Greatest Gardens with Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein
programme.desc: Garden experts Diarmuid Gavin and Carol Klein are joined by guest judge and passionate gardener Dame Prue Leith as they visit three stunning gardens. The first contender is a vibrant three-and-a-half-acre Fernhill garden, lovingly filled with more than 4,000 plants, and owned by Tomas and Thomas in Ballynahinch. Number two is a spectacular suburban garden in Jordanstown created by Karen. And finally, a surprising six-acre farmhouse garden near Crumlin, County Antrim, planted over the last 27 years by Will. Diarmuid, Carol and Prue are searching for a garden which the owners have poured their heart and soul into creating. Which will they decide deserves a place in the final?

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at travel in the deaf community, with tips for deaf travellers, solo adventures around the world and stories of confidence, independence and discovery. Presenter Ahmed Mudawi is joined in the studio by stand-up comedian and keen traveller Leah Francisco. Having travelled widely across Europe and Central America, Leah shares how taking the leap to explore the world helped her regain confidence and embrace new experiences.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The big political issues of the day.

In each episode there are three rounds, and the three highest scorers from each round play against each other in the final. The winner of this battle faces a Â£10,000 question.
